description: Final Paint closes when all interior and exterior paint and stain work is complete, client-approved, and surfaces are protected and ready for fixture and hardware set-out.

items.label: Confirmed interior paint color schedule matches client-signed selection sheet (room-by-room, sheen by surface type)
Confirmed exterior paint and stain colors match client-signed selection sheet (body, trim, door, soffit)
All drywall punch items from Drywall phase are closed — no remaining nail pops, seam ridges, or voids visible under raking light
All trim carpentry complete and sanded — no paint crew asked to caulk over unset nail holes or unfinished joints
HVAC, plumbing, and electrical rough-in inspections passed; walls are closed and will not be re-opened after paint
All windows and doors protected with masking or film prior to painter mobilization
Superintendent confirmed humidity is below 60% and temperature is between 50°F and 90°F at time of application
Painting sub crew size and lead confirmed, schedule locked against trim-out and cabinet install windows
Interior paint sequence scheduled: prime coat → finish coat by zone, sequenced to keep trades out of wet areas
Exterior paint sequence coordinated with weather forecast — no application within 24 hours of projected rain
Palmetto superintendent on-site for first day of both interior and exterior work to verify prep standards
Paint sub has confirmed responsibility for daily cleanup of drop cloths, masking debris, and overspray
All paint, primer, and stain products on-site match the approved spec (brand, product line, sheen, color code per room schedule)
Sufficient material on-site for full job plus minimum 10% overage; extra mixed gallons labeled and staged for touch-up
All caulk products on-site are paintable, interior-grade where required, and exterior-rated (siliconized or equivalent) where required
Wood stain and sealer products confirmed for any stained millwork, beams, or doors per selection sheet
All masking, plastic sheeting, and floor protection materials on-site before crew begins
All surfaces primed with manufacturer-specified primer coat prior to finish coats; prime coat inspected and approved by superintendent before top coat begins
Minimum two finish coats applied to all walls and ceilings; three coats applied to all doors and trim per Palmetto quality standard
No holidays (missed spots), brush drag lines, lap marks, or roller texture inconsistencies visible under natural light inspection at 36-inch distance
All interior caulk lines are tight, uniform width, and fully tooled — no fish-eyes, voids, or pigment bleed at trim-to-wall joints
Exterior paint fully covers all trim end-grain, corner joints, and butt seams; no bare wood exposed
Paint edges at ceiling-to-wall transitions are cut within 1/16 inch, straight, and consistent throughout each room
All painted doors checked for even sheen, no runs or sags on stiles and rails, and hardware bore areas fully covered
All finish-painted surfaces in cabinet, hardware, and fixture zones covered with clean protective film before those trades mobilize
Painted floors (if any, e.g., garage or utility) allowed full cure time per manufacturer spec before traffic allowed
All interior window sills and door frames confirmed free of overspray before paint sub demobilizes
Painter has delivered all leftover labeled paint cans (minimum one quart per color) to Palmetto for client touch-up kit
All masking, tape, plastic, and drop cloths removed from site; no adhesive residue left on glass, hardware, or flooring
Superintendent performs full interior paint walk under raking light (flashlight at 45-degree angle to walls) and logs all deficiencies before client walk
Superintendent performs full exterior paint walk in direct daylight, checking all elevations for coverage, sheen consistency, and caulk integrity
All superintendent-identified paint deficiencies corrected and re-inspected before client walkthrough is scheduled
Client walkthrough completed with superintendent present; all client-noted paint items logged on punch list with room, surface, and description
All client punch items corrected, re-inspected by superintendent, and signed off by client before phase is closed
Paint phase completion confirmed in writing (email or Buildertrend sign-off) by client before cabinet, hardware, or fixture installation begins
